jsx.prompt: Show my overdue invoices in a table with client, amount, and due date, plus a headline total.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoices">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoices" />

    <Stat
      label="Total Overdue"
      value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
      format="money"
      tone="danger"
    />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
      ]}
      sortBy="dueDate"
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Uses invoices.list with overdue filter, formatted money/date columns, headline total Stat, and all data traces to the host tool.

jsx.prompt: Build a dashboard of my bank accounts: a balance stat per account and a small trend for each.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Bank Accounts Dashboard">
  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Bank Accounts" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Live balances and 12-point trends for all your connected accounts." />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <CardList
        items={accounts.data}
        titleKey="name"
        subtitleKey="type"
        valueKey="balanceCents"
        valueFormat="money"
      />
    </Grid>

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Account Trends">
        <Stack gap={6}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Balance sparklines — one per account" />
          <DataTable
            rows={accounts.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "name", label: "Account" },
              { key: "type", label: "Type" },
              { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" },
              { key: "sparkline", label: "Trend" }
            ]}
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Grid columns={3}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Accounts"
        value={accounts.data.length}
        format="number"
      />
    </Grid>
  </Stack>
</App>
```

Wait — let me rethink this. The best approach is a per-account card with a `Stat` + `Sparkline` side by side, iterating over the accounts. Since we can't dynamically loop in markup, I'll use `CardList` for the summary and then a `DataTable` with inline sparklines, plus top-level stats.

```xml
<App name="Bank Accounts Dashboard">
  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />

  <Stack gap={8}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Bank Accounts" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Live balances and 12-point balance trends across all connected accounts." />

    <Divider />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <CardList
        items={accounts.data}
        titleKey="name"
        subtitleKey="type"
        valueKey="balanceCents"
        valueFormat="money"
      />
    </Grid>

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Balance Trends" />

    <DataTable
      rows={accounts.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "name",         label: "Account" },
        { key: "type",         label: "Type" },
        { key: "balanceCents", label: "Current Balance", format: "money" },
        { key: "sparkline",    label: "12-Month Trend" }
      ]}
      emptyLabel="No accounts found."
    />
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: The per-account trend relies on a 'sparkline' field from accounts.list which is not a traceable data source; the actual trend data should come from accounts.transactions, and the wire is messy with duplicate versions and prose.

jsx.prompt: List clients sorted by outstanding balance, and let me filter by client name.

jsx.prompt: Give me a spending breakdown by category for this quarter as a donut plus a table of the numbers.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Spending Breakdown – This Quarter">
  <Query id="spending" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "quarter" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Spending by Category — This Quarter" />

    <Grid columns={2}>
      <Card title="Category Breakdown">
        <Donut
          slices={spending.data}
          label="Total Spending"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Category Totals">
        <DataTable
          rows={spending.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "category", label: "Category" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "pct", label: "% of Total", format: "percent" }
          ]}
          sortBy="amountCents"
          emptyLabel="No spending data available."
        />
      </Card>
    </Grid>

    <Surface>
      <Stat
        label="Total Quarterly Spending"
        value={spending.totalCents}
        format="money"
      />
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Donut uses raw slice numbers, table formats money/percent, stat formatted, all data from spending.byCategory.

jsx.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.

jsx.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Overdue Invoice Reminders">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Review all overdue invoices below and send payment reminders in one click." />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Total Outstanding"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Card
      title="Overdue Invoices"
      description="All invoices currently past their due date."
      tone="danger"
    >
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <DataTable
          rows={overdueInvoices.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "status", label: "Status" }
          ]}
          sortBy="dueDate"
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!"
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Reminders to All Overdue Clients"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Cleanly lists overdue invoices from invoices.list with formatted money/date and wires bulk sendReminders action, fully meeting the ask.

jsx.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Invoice Detail">

  <Query id="invoice" tool="invoices.get" input={{ id: "inv_001" }} />

  <Stack gap={6}>

    <Row gap={4}>
      <Stack gap={1}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Invoice Detail" />
        <Text variant="caption" text={invoice.id} />
      </Stack>
      <EnumBadge value={invoice.status} />
    </Row>

    <Grid columns={3}>
      <Stat label="Amount Due" value={invoice.amountCents} format="money" />
      <Stat label="Due Date" value={invoice.dueDate} format="date" />
      <Stat label="Status" value={invoice.status} />
    </Grid>

    <Divider />

    <Card title="Client" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={2}>
        <Row gap={3}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Name" />
          <Text variant="body" text={invoice.client.name} />
        </Row>
        <Row gap={3}>
          <Text variant="caption" text="Email" />
          <Text variant="body" text={invoice.client.email} />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>

    <Card title="Line Items" tone="default">
      <DataTable
        rows={invoice.lineItems}
        columns={[
          { key: "description", label: "Description" },
          { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
        ]}
        emptyLabel="No line items found."
      />
    </Card>

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={3}>
        <Text variant="heading" text="Actions" />
        <Row gap={3}>
          <Button
            label="Mark as Paid"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="invoices.markPaid"
            payload={{ id: invoice.id }}
          />
          <Button
            label="Send Reminder"
            variant="secondary"
            onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
            payload={{ ids: [invoice.id] }}
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Cleanly renders invoice detail with line items via invoices.get and wires markPaid correctly with proper money/date formatting and raw values traceable to the host tool.

jsx.prompt: Create a clients page with a search box and a results table of matches showing their balances.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Clients">
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" />
  <Query id="searchResults" tool="clients.search" input={{ query: "" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Clients" />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Input
          label="Search Clients"
          name="clientSearch"
          type="search"
          placeholder="Search by name or email…"
          onChange="clients.search"
        />

        <DataTable
          rows={searchResults.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },
            { key: "email", label: "Email" },
            { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" },
          ]}
          filterableBy={["name", "email"]}
          emptyLabel="No clients match your search."
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>

    <Card title="All Clients" description="Full client roster with current balances.">
      <DataTable
        rows={allClients.data}
        columns={[
          { key: "name", label: "Client Name" },
          { key: "email", label: "Email" },
          { key: "tier", label: "Tier" },
          { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" },
        ]}
        sortBy="balanceCents"
        filterableBy={["name", "email", "tier"]}
        emptyLabel="No clients found."
      />
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Search box and results table with money-formatted balances are properly wired to clients.search; minor issue is the search input binding to the query via onChange is a bit awkward but overall correct.

jsx.prompt: Summarize accounts receivable: total overdue, count of overdue invoices, and the worst three clients.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Accounts Receivable Summary">

  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />
  <Query id="allClients" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Accounts Receivable Summary" />

    <Row gap={16}>
      <Stat
        label="Total Overdue"
        value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
        format="money"
        tone="danger"
      />
      <Stat
        label="Overdue Invoices"
        value={overdueInvoices.count}
        format="number"
        tone="danger"
      />
    </Row>

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="Worst 3 Overdue Clients" />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount Overdue", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "status", label: "Status" }
      ]}
      sortBy="amountCents"
      limit={3}
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices"
    />

    <Divider />

    <Text variant="heading" text="All Overdue Invoices" />

    <DataTable
      rows={overdueInvoices.data}
      columns={[
        { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
        { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
        { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
        { key: "status", label: "Status" }
      ]}
      sortBy="amountCents"
      filterableBy={["client.name"]}
      emptyLabel="No overdue invoices"
    />

  </Stack>

</App>

jsx.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Create Payment">
  <Query id="accountsData" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="clientsData" tool="clients.list" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Send a Payment" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="Transfer funds from one of your bank accounts to a client." />

    <Card title="Payment Details" tone="default">
      <Stack gap={20}>
        <Select
          label="From Account"
          name="accountId"
          placeholder="Select an account…"
          options={accountsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Select
          label="Recipient (Client)"
          name="toClientId"
          placeholder="Select a client…"
          options={clientsData.data}
          required={true}
        />

        <Input
          label="Amount"
          name="amountCents"
          type="number"
          placeholder="Enter amount in cents"
          hint="Enter the payment amount in cents (e.g. 5000 = $50.00)"
          required={true}
        />

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Button
            label="Send Payment"
            variant="primary"
            onClick="payments.create"
          />
          <Button
            label="Cancel"
            variant="secondary"
          />
        </Row>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Form has account, recipient, and amount fields sourced from real tools and wired to payments.create write action.

jsx.prompt: Give me an at-a-glance finance overview: cash on hand, this-month revenue, and overdue total.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Finance Overview">

  <Query id="accounts" tool="accounts.list" />
  <Query id="revenue" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Finance Overview" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="A real-time snapshot of your key financial metrics." />

    <Divider />

    <Grid columns={3}>

      <Card tone="accent" title="Cash on Hand">
        <Stat
          label="Total Balance Across All Accounts"
          value={accounts.data}
          format="money"
          tone="accent"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card tone="default" title="This Month's Revenue">
        <Stat
          label="Revenue — Current Month"
          value={revenue.data}
          format="money"
          tone="default"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card tone="danger" title="Overdue Invoices">
        <Stat
          label="Total Overdue Balance"
          value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}
          format="money"
          tone="danger"
          trend={overdueInvoices.count}
        />
      </Card>

    </Grid>

    <Divider />

    <Grid columns={2}>

      <Card title="Account Balances">
        <DataTable
          rows={accounts.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "name", label: "Account" },
            { key: "type", label: "Type" },
            { key: "balanceCents", label: "Balance", format: "money" }
          ]}
          sortBy="balanceCents"
        />
      </Card>

      <Card title="Overdue Invoices">
        <DataTable
          rows={overdueInvoices.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "client.name", label: "Client" },
            { key: "dueDate", label: "Due Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" }
          ]}
          sortBy="dueDate"
          emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — great work!"
        />
      </Card>

    </Grid>

    <Card title="Monthly Revenue Trend">
      <LineChart
        points={revenue.data}
        label="Monthly Revenue (trailing 6 months)"
      />
    </Card>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Addresses all three metrics with correct tools and formatting, but Stat values bind raw arrays (accounts.data, revenue.data) instead of aggregated cents, so cash-on-hand and this-month revenue won't render correct scalars.

jsx.prompt: Show all accounts as cards with name, type, balance, and a sparkline.
jsx.wire:
``` Wait — `CardList` doesn't surface the sparkline. Let me compose proper cards manually so the sparkline renders per account. ```xml ``` That loses the sparkline too. The right move is an Island per card — but Islands are last resort. Since Sparkline is a catalog component and I need one per account row, I'll use a DataTable for the structured fields and pair it with a note, but actually the prompt specifically asks for cards + sparkline. Let me compose this correctly using the available primitives with an Island for the sparkline-in-card layout since no single catalog component covers "card grid with embedded sparklines": ```xml {accountsData.data?.map(account => (
{account.name}
{account.type}
{new Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency: "USD" }).format(account.balanceCents / 100)}
))}

jsx.prompt: Make a page that shows the single largest overdue invoice with a prominent amount and a reminder action.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Largest Overdue Invoice">
  <Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{ status: "overdue" }} />

  <Stack gap={6}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Largest Overdue Invoice" />

    <Card tone="danger" title="Outstanding Payment Required" description="This invoice is past due and requires immediate attention.">
      <Stack gap={5}>
        <Row gap={4}>
          <Stat
            label="Amount Due"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].amountCents}
            format="money"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Due Date"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].dueDate}
            format="date"
            tone="danger"
          />
          <Stat
            label="Client"
            value={overdueInvoices.data[0].client.name}
          />
        </Row>

        <Divider />

        <Row gap={3}>
          <Badge label="Overdue" tone="danger" />
          <Text variant="caption" text={overdueInvoices.data[0].id} />
        </Row>

        <Button
          label="Send Payment Reminder"
          variant="primary"
          onClick="invoices.sendReminders"
          payload={{ ids: [overdueInvoices.data[0].id] }}
        />
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Uses invoices.list without sorting/limit to guarantee the single largest overdue invoice, relying on data[0] which may not be the max amount, though components and action wiring are otherwise correct.

jsx.prompt: Show revenue by month and spending by category side by side for a quick health check.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Financial Health Check">

  <Query id="revenueData" tool="revenue.monthly" />
  <Query id="spendingData" tool="spending.byCategory" input={{ period: "month" }} />

  <Stack gap={24}>

    <Text variant="heading" text="Financial Health Check" />
    <Text variant="caption" text="A snapshot of monthly revenue and spending by category." />

    <Row gap={24}>

      <Card title="Revenue · Last 6 Months" tone="accent">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Revenue"
            value={revenueData.data}
            format="money"
          />
          <LineChart
            points={revenueData.data}
            label="Monthly Revenue"
          />
          <DataTable
            rows={revenueData.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "month", label: "Month" },
              { key: "revenueCents", label: "Revenue", format: "money" }
            ]}
            sortBy="month"
            emptyLabel="No revenue data available."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

      <Card title="Spending · This Month" tone="default">
        <Stack gap={16}>
          <Stat
            label="Total Spending"
            value={spendingData.totalCents}
            format="money"
          />
          <Donut
            slices={spendingData.data}
            label="Spending by Category"
          />
          <DataTable
            rows={spendingData.data}
            columns={[
              { key: "category", label: "Category" },
              { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
              { key: "pct", label: "Share", format: "percent" }
            ]}
            sortBy="amountCents"
            emptyLabel="No spending data available."
          />
        </Stack>
      </Card>

    </Row>

  </Stack>

</App>

judge.notes: Good side-by-side layout with correct tools and formatting, but the Total Revenue Stat binds value to the raw array (revenueData.data) rather than a summed scalar, an awkward/incorrect binding.

jsx.prompt: List recent transactions and let me filter them, showing category as a badge and the amount formatted.
jsx.wire:
<App name="Recent Transactions">
  <Query id="txns" tool="accounts.transactions" />

  <Stack gap={24}>
    <Text variant="heading" text="Recent Transactions" />

    <Surface>
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Row gap={12}>
          <Input
            label="Search"
            name="search"
            placeholder="Filter by description or category…"
          />
          <Select
            label="Category"
            name="category"
            options={[
              { value: "", label: "All Categories" },
              { value: "income", label: "Income" },
              { value: "software", label: "Software" },
              { value: "payroll", label: "Payroll" },
              { value: "travel", label: "Travel" },
              { value: "utilities", label: "Utilities" },
              { value: "marketing", label: "Marketing" },
              { value: "other", label: "Other" },
            ]}
          />
        </Row>

        <Divider />

        <DataTable
          rows={txns.data}
          columns={[
            { key: "date", label: "Date", format: "date" },
            { key: "description", label: "Description" },
            {
              key: "category",
              label: "Category",
            },
            { key: "amountCents", label: "Amount", format: "money" },
          ]}
          sortBy="date"
          filterableBy={["description", "category"]}
          emptyLabel="No transactions found."
        />
      </Stack>
    </Surface>
  </Stack>
</App>

judge.notes: Lists transactions with filtering and formatted money/date, but the category column renders as plain text rather than the requested badge.

fork.prompt: Show the last few transactions on my operating account with amounts and dates.

fork.prompt: I want to send payment reminders for all overdue invoices from one screen.
fork.wire: <App name="Generated"><Query id="overdueInvoices" tool="invoices.list" input={{"status":"overdue"}}/><Stack><Row align="center" justify="space-between"><Text text="Overdue Invoice Reminders" variant="heading"/><Button label="Send All Reminders" variant="primary" onClick={{action:"invoices.sendReminders",payload:{value:overdueInvoices.data}}}/></Row><Divider/><Row><Stat label="Overdue Invoices" tone="danger" value={overdueInvoices.count}/><Stat label="Total Overdue Amount" format="money" tone="danger" value={overdueInvoices.totalCents}/></Row><Card><DataTable columns={[{"key":"client.name","label":"Client"},{"key":"amountCents","label":"Amount","format":"money"},{"key":"dueDate","label":"Due Date","format":"date"},{"key":"status","label":"Status"}]} sortBy="dueDate" filterableBy={["client.name"]} emptyLabel="No overdue invoices — you're all caught up!" rows={overdueInvoices.data}/></Card></Stack></App>

fork.prompt: Show a monthly revenue trend line for the last few months with a headline of the latest month.

fork.prompt: Build an invoice detail view for a single invoice including its line items and a mark-paid action.
fork.wire: <App name="Generated"><Query id="invoiceDetail" tool="invoices.get"/><Stack gap="lg"><Card title="Invoice Detail"><Row gap="md" align="center"><Stack gap="xs"><Text variant="heading" text={invoiceDetail.client.name}/><Text variant="caption" text={invoiceDetail.client.email}/></Stack><EnumBadge kind="invoiceStatus" value={invoiceDetail.status}/></Row><Divider/><Row gap="lg"><Stat label="Invoice Total" format="money" value={invoiceDetail.amountCents}/><Stat label="Due Date" format="date" value={invoiceDetail.dueDate}/><Stat label="Invoice ID" value={invoiceDetail.id}/></Row></Card><Card title="Line Items"><DataTable columns={[{"key":"description","label":"Description"},{"key":"amountCents","label":"Amount","format":"money"}]} emptyLabel="No line items found." rowKey="description" rows={invoiceDetail.lineItems}/></Card><Row justify="flex-end" gap="md"><Button label="Send Payment Reminder" variant="secondary" onClick={{action:"invoices.sendReminders",payload:{value:invoiceDetail.id}}}/><Button label="Mark as Paid" variant="primary" onClick={{action:"invoices.markPaid",payload:{value:invoiceDetail.id}}}/></Row></Stack></App>

judge.notes: Complete invoice detail with line items table, formatted money/date stats, and correctly wired markPaid action bound to real tool data.

fork.prompt: Summarize accounts receivable: total overdue, count of overdue invoices, and the worst three clients.

fork.prompt: Show a payments form to pay a client from an account, with the amount and recipient.

fork.prompt: Show overdue invoices grouped visually with a reminder button per row.
fork.wire:
import React from "react"; interface Invoice { id: string; client: { id: string; name: string }; amountCents: number; dueDate: string; status: string; } interface Props { rows: Invoice[]; onSendReminder: (ids: string[]) => void; } function formatMoney(cents: number) { return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency: "USD" }).format(cents / 100); } function formatDate(iso: string) { return new Date(iso).toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "short", day: "numeric" }); } function daysOverdue(iso: string) { const due = new Date(iso).getTime(); const now = Date.now(); return Math.max(0, Math.floor((now - due) / 86400000)); } export default function OverdueInvoiceRows({ rows = [], onSendReminder }: Props) { const [loadingId, setLoadingId] = React.useState(null); const [sentIds, setSentIds] = React.useState>(new Set()); async function handleReminder(id: string) { setLoadingId(id); try { await onSendReminder([id]); setSentIds(prev => new Set([...prev, id])); } finally { setLoadingId(null); } } if (!rows.length) { return (
🎉 No overdue invoices right now.
); } return (
{rows.map((inv) => { const overdueDays = daysOverdue(inv.dueDate); const sent = sentIds.has(inv.id); const loading = loadingId === inv.id; return (
{/* Left: client + id */}
{inv.client.name} Invoice #{inv.id}
{/* Center: due date + overdue badge */}
Due {formatDate(inv.dueDate)} {overdueDays}d overdue
{/* Right: amount + button */}
{formatMoney(inv.amountCents)}
); })}
); } import React from "react"; interface Invoice { id: string; client: { id: string; name: string }; amountCents: number; dueDate: string; status: string; } interface Props { rows: Invoice[]; onSendReminder: (ids: string[]) => void; } function formatMoney(cents: number) { return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency: "USD" }).format(cents / 100); } function formatDate(iso: string) { return new Date(iso).toLocaleDateString("en-US", { year: "numeric", month: "short", day: "numeric" }); } function daysOverdue(iso: string) { const due = new Date(iso).getTime(); const now = Date.now(); return Math.max(0, Math.floor((now - due) / 86400000)); } export default function OverdueInvoiceRows({ rows = [], onSendReminder }: Props) { const [loadingId, setLoadingId] = React.useState(null); const [sentIds, setSentIds] = React.useState>(new Set()); async function handleReminder(id: string) { setLoadingId(id); try { await onSendReminder([id]); setSentIds(prev => new Set([...prev, id])); } finally { setLoadingId(null); } } if (!rows.length) { return (
🎉 No overdue invoices right now.
); } return (
{rows.map((inv) => { const overdueDays = daysOverdue(inv.dueDate); const sent = sentIds.has(inv.id); const loading = loadingId === inv.id; return (
{inv.client.name} Invoice #{inv.id}
Due {formatDate(inv.dueDate)} {overdueDays}d overdue
{formatMoney(inv.amountCents)}
); })}
); }

judge.notes: Cleanly shows overdue invoices with per-row reminder buttons wired to invoices.sendReminders and all data from invoices.list; duplicate Island definition is a minor redundancy.

fork.prompt: Show all accounts as cards with name, type, balance, and a sparkline.
fork.wire:
import React from "react"; interface Account { id: string; name: string; type: string; balanceCents: number; sparkline: number[]; } interface Props { accounts: Account[]; } function formatMoney(cents: number) { return new Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency: "USD", minimumFractionDigits: 2, }).format(cents / 100); } function MiniSparkline({ values }: { values: number[] }) { if (!values || values.length === 0) return null; const w = 120, h = 40, pad = 4; const min = Math.min(...values); const max = Math.max(...values); const range = max - min || 1; const pts = values.map((v, i) => { const x = pad + (i / (values.length - 1)) * (w - pad * 2); const y = pad + (1 - (v - min) / range) * (h - pad * 2); return `${x},${y}`; }); const polyline = pts.join(" "); const last = pts[pts.length - 1].split(","); const trend = values[values.length - 1] >= values[0]; return ( ); } function typeLabel(type: string) { return type.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + type.slice(1).toLowerCase(); } function typeBadgeStyle(type: string): React.CSSProperties { const map: Record = { checking: "#3b82f6", savings: "#8b5cf6", credit: "#f59e0b", investment: "#10b981", }; return { backgroundColor: map[type.toLowerCase()] ?? "#6b7280", color: "#fff", fontSize: "0.7rem", fontWeight: 600, borderRadius: "9999px", padding: "2px 10px", display: "inline-block", letterSpacing: "0.04em", textTransform: "uppercase", }; } export default function AccountCards({ accounts }: Props) { if (!accounts || accounts.length === 0) { return

No accounts found.

; } return (
{accounts.map((acct) => (
{/* Name + type */}
{acct.name} {typeLabel(acct.type)}
{/* Balance */}
Balance
{formatMoney(acct.balanceCents)}
{/* Sparkline */}
))}
); }

judge.notes: Cleanly renders account cards with name, type, formatted balance, and sparkline from accounts.list, though the sparkline relies on an assumed 'sparkline' field whose provenance from the tool is uncertain.
